titleStmt.title: Letter from Annie MacDonald to Officer of Estates, 1 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Annie MacDonald

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Annie MacDonald to Officer of Estates regarding the "Taylor Estate", which she claims was her late mother's possession. MacDonald does not have access to any paperwork supporting her claim and seeks advice.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Sacremento, Cali., U.S.A. June, 1, 1916. To Officer of Unsettled Estates, Dublin, Ireland. Dear Sir: I am informed that there
is an unsettled estate in Ireland
known as the Taylor Estate, and
I am desirous of obtaining infor-
mation concerning the same. Just-
before my mother's death a good
many years ago she received notice
of it, but the matter was not at-
tended to at the time, nor have I
access to the papers which came
into her possession. If you can give me any
information regarding this mat-
ter, I assure you it will be highly
appreciated. Respectfully yours,
(Miss) Annie L. MacDonald 612 M St.,
Sacremento, California, U.S.A.
